fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Idaho Licensed Dietitian (LD) Application — Requirements & Fees (2026)
seo.description: Apply for an Idaho Licensed Dietitian credential through DOPL. $100 application fee. Master's degree, 1,000 hours supervised practice, and CDR exam required.
seo.keywords: Idaho dietitian license, ID licensed dietitian requirements, Idaho DOPL Board of Medicine Dietitians, Idaho dietitian application fee

board.name: Dietetic Licensure Board
board.parent_dept: Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses (DOPL)

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (transitions from annual $60 to biennial $120 starting mid-2025)

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The credential to practice dietetics in Idaho, regulated by the Idaho Dietetic Licensure Board under DOPL's Board of Medicine.

license_types.title: Provisional Dietitian
license_types.description: Available for applicants who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the CDR exam. Valid for one year, costs $50, and allows practice under the supervision of a Licensed Dietitian.

steps.title: Complete Education & Supervised Practice
steps.description: Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,000 hours of board-approved supervised practice through an accredited dietitian internship.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status satisfies exam requirements.

steps.title: Submit the Application to DOPL
steps.description: Complete the application form online or via paper. Have it notarized, and submit it with the $100 fee to DOPL.

steps.title: Provide Verification Documents
steps.description: Submit a copy of your active CDR card or ensure verification is sent directly. A current CDR card generally confirms both education and examination completion.

documents.detail: Application form with your notarized signature (if using paper form).
Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree (if not using CDR as sole verification).

provisional_text: Idaho offers a Provisional License for $50 for individuals who have fulfilled the educational and supervised practice requirements but have not yet passed the CDR exam. A Provisional Licensed Dietitian must practice under the direction and supervision of an Idaho Licensed Dietitian. The provisional license is valid for one year.
reciprocity_text: Idaho allows for licensure by endorsement. Out-of-state applicants with a current, valid license from a state with equivalent standards or those holding active CDR registration can apply by submitting the standard application, fee, and proof of out-of-state licensure.

faqs.question: How much does an Idaho dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $100. A provisional license costs $50.

faqs.question: When does the Idaho license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Starting in mid-2025, Idaho dietitian licenses transition to a biennial (two-year) renewal cycle based on the licensee's birth year. The biennial renewal fee is $120.

faqs.question: Is the Dietitian Licensure Compact active in Idaho?
faqs.answer: As of early 2026, Idaho has pending legislation (S1284) to adopt the Dietitian Licensure Compact. If enacted, it would allow eligible dietitians to practice across state lines with compact privilege.
